title: Mundaka 3, Khanda 1
summary: Distinguishing between ritualistic knowledge (Apara Vidya) and knowledge of the imperishable Brahman (Para Vidya). Addresses seeking true wisdom, academic stress, and religious confusion.

sections.summary: Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, devotion. The passage moves from “Two birds, united always and known by the same name, closely cling to the same tree. One of them eats the sweet fruit; the other looks on…” through “Truth alone prevails, not falsehood. By truth the path is laid out, the Way of the Gods, on which the seers, whose every desire is satisfied,…” through “Whatever world a man of pure understanding envisages in his mind and whatever desires he cherishes, that world he conquers and those desires he…”

attribution.edition: Three Mundakas, each divided into two Khandas

verses.sanskrit: द्वा सुपर्णा सयुजा सखाया समानं वृक्षं परिषस्वजाते। तयोरन्यः पिप्पलं स्वाद्वत्त्यनश्नन्नन्यो अभिचाकशीति
verses.translation: Two birds, united always and known by the same name, closely cling to the same tree. One of them eats the sweet fruit; the other looks on without eating.

verses.sanskrit: समाने वृक्षे पुरूषो निमग्नोऽनीशया शोचति मुह्यमानः। जुष्टं यदा पश्यत्यन्यमीशमस्य महिमानमिति वीतशोकः
verses.translation: Seated on the same tree, the jiva moans, bewildered by his impotence. But when he beholds the other, the Lord worshipped by all and His glory, he then becomes free from grief.

verses.sanskrit: यदा पश्यः पश्यते रुक्मवर्णं कर्तारमीशं पुरूषं ब्रह्मयोनिम्। तदा विद्वान्पुण्यपापे विधूय विरञ्जनः परमं साम्यमुपैति
verses.translation: When the seer beholds the self-luminous Creator, the Lord, the Purusha, the progenitor of Brahma, then he, the wise seer, shakes off good and evil, becomes stainless and reaches the supreme unity.

verses.sanskrit: प्राणो ह्येष यः सर्वभूतैर्विभाति विजानन्विद्वान्भवते नातिवादी। आत्मक्रीड आत्मरतिः क्रियावानेष ब्रह्मविदां वरिष्ठः
verses.translation: He indeed is Prana; He shines forth variously in all beings. The wise man who knows Him does not babble. Revelling in the Self, delighting in the Self, performing actions, he is the foremost among the knowers of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: सत्येन लभ्यस्तपसा ह्येष आत्मा सम्यग्ज्ञानेन ब्रह्मचर्येण नित्यम्। अन्तःशरीरे ज्योतिर्मयो हि शुभ्रो यं पश्यन्ति यतयः क्षीणदोषाः
verses.translation: This Atman, resplendent and pure, whom the sinless sannyasins behold residing within the body, is attained by unceasing practice of truthfulness, austerity, right knowledge and continence

verses.sanskrit: सत्यमेव जयते नानृतं सत्येन पन्था विततो देवयानः। येनाक्रमन्त्यृषयो ह्याप्तकामा यत्र तत्सत्यस्य परमं निधानम्
verses.translation: Truth alone prevails, not falsehood. By truth the path is laid out, the Way of the Gods, on which the seers, whose every desire is satisfied, proceed to the Highest Abode of the True.

verses.sanskrit: बृहच्च तद्दिव्यमचिन्त्यरूपं सूक्ष्माच्च तत्सूक्ष्मतरं विभाति। दूरात्सुदूरे तदिहान्तिके च पश्यत्स्विहैव निहितं गुहायाम्
verses.translation: That Brahman shines forth, vast, self-luminous, inconceivable, subtler than the subtle. He is far beyond what is far and yet here very near at hand. Verily, He is seen here, dwelling in the cave of the heart of conscious beings.

verses.sanskrit: न चक्षुषा गृह्यते नापि वाचा नान्यैर्देवैस्तपसा कर्मणा वा। ज्ञानप्रसादेन विशुद्धसत्त्वस्ततस्तु तं पश्यते निष्कलं ध्यायमानः
verses.translation: Brahman is not grasped by the eye, nor by speech, nor by the other senses, nor by penance or good works. A man becomes pure through serenity of intellect; thereupon, in meditation, he beholds Him who is without parts.

verses.sanskrit: एषोऽणुरात्मा चेतसा वेदितव्यो यस्मिन्प्राणः पञ्चधा संविवेश। प्राणैश्चित्तं सर्वमोतं प्रजानां यस्मिन्विशुद्धे विभवत्येष आत्मा
verses.translation: That subtle Atman is to be known by the intellect here in the body where the prana has entered fivefold. By Atman the intellects of men are pervaded, together with the senses. When the intellect is purified, Atman shines forth.

verses.sanskrit: यं यं लोकं मनसा संविभाति विशुद्धसत्त्वः कामयते यांश्च कामान्। तं तं लोकं जयते तांश्च कामांस्तस्मादात्मज्ञं ह्यर्चयेद्भूतिकामः
verses.translation: Whatever world a man of pure understanding envisages in his mind and whatever desires he cherishes, that world he conquers and those desires he obtains, Therefore let everyone who wants prosperity worship the man who knows the Self.
